---
title: "ODOT Safety Analysis"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(sf)
library(leaflet)
library(tidyverse)
setwd("~/2025 - 2026 XU/project")

### Read Road_inventory

road_inventory <- st_read("Road_Inventory.gdb", layer = "RoadInventory")

### Reading and combining Crash Data

Crash2020 <- read.csv("crashes/2020Crash.csv")
Crash2021 <- read.csv("crashes/2021Crash.csv")
Crash2022 <- read.csv("crashes/2022Crash.csv")
Crash2023 <- read.csv("crashes/2023Crash.csv")
Crash2024 <- read.csv("crashes/2024Crash.csv")

crashes_all <- rbind(
  Crash2020,
  Crash2021,
  Crash2022,
  Crash2023,
  Crash2024
)

### Read in Projects

All_Projects <- read.csv("All_Projects.csv")
head(All_Projects)

### Reading in PCR

st_layers("PCR_Local.gdb")
PCR_Local <- st_read("PCR_Local.gdb", layer = "PCR_Local")

st_layers("PCR_State.gdb")
PCR_State <- st_read("PCR_State.gdb", layer = "PCRState")

# Original ID columns list
id_cols <- c("PCR_HISTORY_ID", "ROUTE_NBR", "ROUTE_SUFFIX", "NLFID", "COUNTY_CD",
             "JURISDICTION", "ROUTE_TYPE", "DIRECTION_CD", "LEFT_MUNI_NAME", 
             "CITY_NAME", "LEFT_TWP_NAME", "TOWNSHIP_NAME")

# Keep only columns that exist in the dataframe
id_cols_exist <- intersect(id_cols, colnames(PCR_Local))

# Function to safely convert character to numeric except ID columns
make_numeric_safe <- function(df, exclude_cols = id_cols_exist) {
  df %>%
    mutate(across(.cols = where(is.character) & !all_of(exclude_cols), 
                  ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))
}

# Apply to both datasets
PCR_Local_fixed <- make_numeric_safe(PCR_Local)
PCR_State_fixed <- make_numeric_safe(PCR_State)

PCR_combined <- bind_rows(
  PCR_Local_fixed %>% mutate(Source = "Local"),
  PCR_State_fixed %>% mutate(Source = "State")
)

#write.csv(crashes_all, "crashes_all.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#write.csv(PCR_combined, "PCR_combined.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#write.csv(road_inventory, "road_inventory.csv", row.names = FALSE)

head(road_inventory)


############################################
### BIG JOIN: CRASHES + ROAD INVENTORY + PCR
############################################

### ----------------------------------------
### 1. LOAD CORE DATASETS
### ----------------------------------------

# Crashes
crashes <- crashes_all

# PCR
pcr <- PCR_combined

### ----------------------------------------
### 2. CONVERT CRASHES TO SPATIAL FEATURES
### ----------------------------------------

# Convert crash table to sf points using lat/long
crashes_sf <- st_as_sf(
  crashes,
  coords = c("ODOT_LONGITUDE_NBR", "ODOT_LATITUDE_NBR"),
  crs = 4326,
  remove = FALSE
)

# Check CRS consistency
st_crs(crashes_sf)
st_crs(road_inventory)

# Project crashes to match road inventory CRS (EPSG:3857)
crashes_sf <- st_transform(crashes_sf, 3857)

# Drop Z/M dimensions (required for GEOS spatial joins)
crashes_sf <- st_zm(crashes_sf, drop = TRUE, what = "ZM")
road_inventory <- st_zm(road_inventory, drop = TRUE, what = "ZM")

### ----------------------------------------
### 3. SPATIAL JOIN: CRASHES → ROAD SEGMENTS
### ----------------------------------------

# Match each crash to its nearest road inventory segment
crash_segment <- st_join(
  crashes_sf,
  road_inventory,
  join = st_nearest_feature
)

# Quick sanity check
head(crash_segment)

# Optional export
#write.csv(crash_segment, "crash_segment.csv", row.names = FALSE)

### ----------------------------------------
### 4. AGGREGATE CRASHES TO SEGMENT LEVEL
### ----------------------------------------

# Collapse crash-level data into one row per road segment
segment_crash_summary <- crash_segment %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    total_crashes = n(),
    fatal_crashes = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_CD == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    serious_injury_crashes = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_CD == 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    injury_crashes = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_CD %in% 1:4, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Optional export
# write.csv(crash_segment, "crash_segment.csv", row.names = FALSE)


### ----------------------------------------
### 5. JOIN CRASH COUNTS BACK TO ROAD NETWORK
### ----------------------------------------
road_with_crashes <- road_inventory %>%
  left_join(
    segment_crash_summary,
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  )

road_with_crashes <- road_with_crashes %>%
  mutate(
    total_crashes = replace_na(total_crashes, 0),
    fatal_crashes = replace_na(fatal_crashes, 0),
    serious_injury_crashes = replace_na(serious_injury_crashes, 0),
    injury_crashes = replace_na(injury_crashes, 0)
  )


### ----------------------------------------
### 6. PREPARE PCR DATA (LATEST PER SEGMENT)
### ----------------------------------------

# Drop geometry for attribute join
PCR_df <- st_drop_geometry(PCR_combined)
class(PCR_df)

# Align key name with road inventory
PCR_df <- PCR_df %>%
  rename(NLF_ID = NLFID)

# Keep only the most recent PCR observation per segment
PCR_latest <- PCR_df %>%
  group_by(NLF_ID) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = PCR_YEAR, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()


### ----------------------------------------
### 7. JOIN PCR DATA TO ROAD + CRASH DATA
### ----------------------------------------

final_sf <- road_with_crashes %>%
  left_join(
    PCR_latest,
    by = "NLF_ID"
  )

# Verify row count preserved (1 row per segment)
nrow(final_sf) == nrow(road_with_crashes)


### ----------------------------------------
### 8. COMPUTE CRASH RATES
### ----------------------------------------

# Crashes per mile
final_sf <- final_sf %>%
  mutate(
    crashes_per_mile = total_crashes / SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR
  )

# Million Vehicle Miles Traveled (MVMT) and crash rate
final_sf <- final_sf %>%
  mutate(
    MVMT = (AADT_TOTAL * 365 * SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR) / 1e6,
    crashes_per_MVMT = total_crashes / MVMT
  )


### ----------------------------------------
### 9. SANITY CHECKS
### ----------------------------------------

summary(final_sf$total_crashes)
summary(final_sf$crashes_per_mile)
summary(final_sf$crashes_per_MVMT)

sum(is.infinite(final_sf$crashes_per_MVMT))

segment_crash_summary <- crash_segment %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    total_crashes = n(),
    fatal_crashes = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_TXT == "Fatal", na.rm = TRUE),
    injury_crashes = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_CD %in% c(2,3,4), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

road_with_crashes <- road_inventory %>%
  left_join(segment_crash_summary, by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID") %>%
  mutate(
    total_crashes = replace_na(total_crashes, 0),
    fatal_crashes = replace_na(fatal_crashes, 0),
    injury_crashes = replace_na(injury_crashes, 0)
  )

road_with_crashes <- road_with_crashes %>%
  mutate(
    crashes_per_mile = total_crashes / SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR,
    crashes_per_MVMT = (total_crashes * 1e6) /
      (ADT_TOTAL_NBR * 365 * SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR)
  )


### ----------------------------------------
### 10. EXPORT FINAL DATASET
### ----------------------------------------

final_sf <- road_with_crashes %>%
  left_join(PCR_latest, by = "NLF_ID")


#Sanity Checks
summary(road_with_crashes$crashes_per_mile)
summary(road_with_crashes$crashes_per_MVMT)

sum(is.na(road_with_crashes$crashes_per_MVMT))
sum(is.infinite(road_with_crashes$crashes_per_MVMT))

##write.csv(final_sf, "final_sf", row.names = FALSE)

##write.csv(
  #final_sf[1:25000, ],
  #"final_sf_first_25k.csv",
 # row.names = FALSE
#)

############################################
### ANALYSIS-READY SUBSET
############################################

analysis_df <- final_sf %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(ADT_TOTAL_NBR),
    ADT_TOTAL_NBR > 0,
    SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR > 0
  )


library(lubridate)

projects_filtered <- All_Projects %>%
  mutate(
    END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT = as.Date(END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT),
    year(END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT) >= 2021,
    year(END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT) <= 2023
  )

nrow(projects_filtered)

temp_join <- analysis_df %>%
  inner_join(projects_filtered, by = "NLF_ID")

names(temp_join)

treated_segments <- temp_join %>%
  filter(
    CTL_BEGIN_NBR <= CTL_END.y,
    CTL_END_NBR >= CTL_BEGIN.y
  )

nrow(treated_segments)

treated_ids <- treated_segments %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID)

nrow(treated_ids)

analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    treated = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% treated_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  )
table(analysis_df$treated)

summary(as.Date(treated_segments$END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT))
```

```{r}
############################################
### yearly crash rates per segment
############################################

segment_year_crashes <- crash_segment %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, CRASH_YR) %>%
  summarise(
    yearly_crashes = n(),
    yearly_fatal = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_CD == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    yearly_injury = sum(CRASH_SEVERITY_CD %in% c(2,3,4), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

head(segment_year_crashes)

segment_year_data <- segment_year_crashes %>%
  left_join(
    analysis_df %>%
      st_drop_geometry() %>%
      select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID,
             SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR,
             ADT_TOTAL_NBR),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  )

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  mutate(
    MVMT = (ADT_TOTAL_NBR * 365 * SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR) / 1e6,
    crash_rate_MVMT = yearly_crashes / MVMT
  )

summary(segment_year_data$crash_rate_MVMT)

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      CRASH_YR %in% c(2020, 2021) ~ "before",
      CRASH_YR %in% c(2023, 2024) ~ "after",
      TRUE ~ "transition"
    )
  )

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(period != "transition")

table(segment_year_data$CRASH_YR)

treated_ids <- treated_segments %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID)

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  mutate(
    treated = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% treated_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  )

table(segment_year_data$treated)

did_summary <- segment_year_data %>%
  group_by(treated, period) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_crash_rate = median(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    observations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

did_summary

did_effect <- did_summary %>%
  select(treated, period, avg_crash_rate) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = period,
    values_from = avg_crash_rate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    change = after - before
  )

did_effect

### did_effect: 1.37 based on this sample of 2021–2023 projects, treated 
## segments did not show greater crash reduction than untreated segments.

```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
############################################
### Projects using Safety Funds
############################################



projects_safety <- projects_filtered %>%
  filter(SAFETY_FUNDS_IND == "Y")

nrow(projects_safety)

treated_safety_segments <- analysis_df %>%
  inner_join(projects_safety, by = "NLF_ID") %>%
  filter(
    CTL_BEGIN_NBR <= CTL_END.y,
    CTL_END_NBR >= CTL_BEGIN.y
  )

treated_safety_ids <- treated_safety_segments %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID)

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  mutate(
    treated_safety =
      ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in%
      treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  )

table(segment_year_data$treated_safety)

did_summary_safety <- segment_year_data %>%
  group_by(treated_safety, period) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_crash_rate = median(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    observations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

did_effect_safety <- did_summary_safety %>%
  select(treated_safety, period, avg_crash_rate) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = period,
    values_from = avg_crash_rate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    change = after - before
  )

did_safety_effect_value <-
  did_effect_safety$change[did_effect_safety$treated_safety == TRUE] -
  did_effect_safety$change[did_effect_safety$treated_safety == FALSE]
did_safety_effect_value

### also suggests that construction isnt helping and making it worse
### raises the question do safety projects show immediate improvements, or is there a lag effect
```

```{r}
############################################
### Crash Rate Around Safety Project Completion
############################################

projects_safety <- projects_safety %>%
  mutate(
    completion_year = year(as.Date(END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(completion_year))

treated_safety_segments <- analysis_df %>%
  inner_join(projects_safety, by = "NLF_ID") %>%
  filter(
    CTL_BEGIN_NBR <= CTL_END.y,
    CTL_END_NBR >= CTL_BEGIN.y
  ) %>%
  select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, completion_year) %>%
  distinct()

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  left_join(
    treated_safety_segments,
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  )

segment_year_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  mutate(
    event_time = CRASH_YR - completion_year
  )

event_study_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(event_time),
    event_time >= -2,
    event_time <= 2
  )

event_summary <- event_study_data %>%
  group_by(event_time) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    observations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(event_summary, aes(x = event_time, y = avg_crash_rate)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Crash Rate Around Safety Project Completion",
    x = "Years Relative to Project Completion",
    y = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
############################################
### Event Study: Treated vs Control
############################################

# segment_year_data already has completion_year and event_time from above
# DO NOT re-join treated_safety_segments here — it was already joined

event_window <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(
    treated_safety == TRUE,
    event_time >= -2,
    event_time <= 2
  )

# For control, anchor their "event time" to the median completion year of treated projects
median_completion_year <- median(
  treated_safety_segments$completion_year, na.rm = TRUE
)

control_window <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(treated_safety == FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    event_time = CRASH_YR - median_completion_year  # <-- THIS was the bug
  ) %>%
  filter(event_time >= -2, event_time <= 2)

event_study_df <- bind_rows(
  event_window %>% mutate(group = "Treated (Safety)"),
  control_window %>% mutate(group = "Control")
)

event_summary <- event_study_df %>%
  group_by(group, event_time) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(event_summary, aes(x = event_time, y = avg_crash_rate, color = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = -2:2) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 20),
    breaks = 5:18
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Event Study: Crash Rate Around Safety Project Completion",
    subtitle = "t = 0 is the year construction completed",
    x = "Years Relative to Project Completion",
    y = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

library(lfe)

did_model <- felm(
  crash_rate_MVMT ~ treated_safety * I(period == "after") |
    ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID + CRASH_YR |
    0 |
    ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID,
  data = segment_year_data
)
summary(did_model)

#Safety-funded segments showed essentially zero additional change in crash rate compared to control segments
#The effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero
#This confirms your raw DiD finding

# Keep only segments observed in BOTH before and after periods
balanced_segments <- segment_year_data %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
  filter(all(c("before", "after") %in% period)) %>%
  ungroup()

nrow(balanced_segments)  # check how many remain

did_model_balanced <- felm(
  crash_rate_MVMT ~ treated_safety * I(period == "after") |
    ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID + CRASH_YR |
    0 |
    ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID,
  data = balanced_segments
)

summary(did_model_balanced)

### no noticable changes

# Clean event study - treated segments only, no re-joining
# Clean event study - treated segments only, no re-joining
event_summary <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(
    treated_safety == TRUE,
    !is.na(event_time),
    event_time >= -2,
    event_time <= 2
  ) %>%
  group_by(event_time) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(event_summary)

ggplot(event_summary, aes(x = event_time, y = avg_crash_rate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = avg_crash_rate - 1.96*se,
                  ymax = avg_crash_rate + 1.96*se),
              alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = max(event_summary$avg_crash_rate),
           label = "Construction\nComplete", hjust = 0, color = "red", size = 5.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = -2:2) +
  labs(
    title = "Event Study: Crash Rates Around Safety Project Completion",
    subtitle = "Treated segments only | t=0 is completion year",
    x = "Years Relative to Project Completion",
    y = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# Filter to only segments where construction completed in 2021
# This gives us 3 full years of post-completion data (2022, 2023, 2024)

# Filter to only segments where construction completed in 2021
# This gives us 3 full years of post-completion data (2022, 2023, 2024)

cohort_2021 <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(
    treated_safety == TRUE,
    !is.na(completion_year),
    completion_year == 2021
  ) %>%
  mutate(event_time = CRASH_YR - 2021)

# Check what years we have
table(cohort_2021$CRASH_YR)
print(paste("Number of segments:", n_distinct(cohort_2021$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID)))

cohort_summary <- cohort_2021 %>%
  filter(event_time >= -2, event_time <= 3) %>%  # extend to +3 for lag testing
  group_by(event_time) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(cohort_summary)

ggplot(cohort_summary, aes(x = event_time, y = avg_crash_rate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = avg_crash_rate - 1.96*se,
                  ymax = avg_crash_rate + 1.96*se),
              alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkgreen") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = max(cohort_summary$avg_crash_rate),
           label = "Construction\nComplete", hjust = 0, color = "red", size = 5.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = -2:3) +
  labs(
    title = "2021 Cohort: Crash Rates After Safety Project Completion",
    subtitle = "Only segments completing construction in 2021 | Extends to t=+3",
    x = "Years Relative to Project Completion (2021)",
    y = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



# Compare baseline characteristics of treated vs control
baseline_check <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    treated_safety = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  ) %>%
  group_by(treated_safety) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n(),
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_length = mean(SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crashes_per_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_PCR = mean(PCR_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(baseline_check)

### Compared roads had bias so we need to reapproach

# Step 1: Get the ADT range of treated segments
treated_adt_range <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    treated_safety = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  ) %>%
  filter(treated_safety == TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    low  = quantile(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
    high = quantile(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(treated_adt_range)
# Expected output: low = 573, high = 39364

# Step 2: Filter to comparable roads only
matched_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(
    treated_safety == TRUE |
    (treated_safety == FALSE &
     ADT_TOTAL_NBR >= treated_adt_range$low &
     ADT_TOTAL_NBR <= treated_adt_range$high)
  )

table(matched_data$treated_safety)
# Expected: FALSE ~155k, TRUE ~14k

# Step 3: DiD summary on matched sample
did_summary_matched <- matched_data %>%
  group_by(treated_safety, period) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

matched_data <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(
    treated_safety == TRUE |
      (treated_safety == FALSE &
         ADT_TOTAL_NBR >= treated_adt_range$low &
         ADT_TOTAL_NBR <= treated_adt_range$high)
  )

table(matched_data$treated_safety)

did_summary_matched <- matched_data %>%
  group_by(treated_safety, period) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(did_summary_matched)

did_plot_data <- data.frame(
  period = c("Before", "After", "Before", "After"),
  group = c("Control", "Control", "Treated (Safety-Funded)", "Treated (Safety-Funded)"),
  avg_crash_rate = c(10.6, 10.3, 7.65, 7.42)
) %>%
  mutate(period = factor(period, levels = c("Before", "After")))

ggplot(did_plot_data, aes(x = period, y = avg_crash_rate, 
                          color = group, group = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_label(aes(label = round(avg_crash_rate, 2)), 
             nudge_x = 0.08, show.legend = FALSE, size = 5.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "gray50", 
                                "Treated (Safety-Funded)" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    title = "Difference-in-Differences: Safety Project Impact on Crash Rates",
    subtitle = "Matched on ADT volume | DiD effect = +0.07 (not significant)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#################################
### Looking at PRIMARY_WORK_TYPE
#################################

# Step 1: Create projects_safety_typed
projects_safety_typed <- projects_safety %>%
  select(NLF_ID, CTL_BEGIN, CTL_END, completion_year, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY)

# Step 1: do the join first and check what columns exist
test_join <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  inner_join(projects_safety_typed, by = "NLF_ID", relationship = "many-to-many")

# Check if CTL_BEGIN/CTL_END survived the join
names(test_join)[grep("CTL", names(test_join))]

treated_typed_segments <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  inner_join(projects_safety_typed, by = "NLF_ID", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  filter(
    CTL_BEGIN_NBR <= CTL_END.y,
    CTL_END_NBR >= CTL_BEGIN.y
  ) %>%
  select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, completion_year, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY) %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Check project type counts
category_counts <- treated_typed_segments %>%
  count(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, sort = TRUE)

View(category_counts)

# Categories with enough data to analyze
key_categories <- c(
  "Traffic Control (Safety)",
  "Roadway Minor Rehab", 
  "Intersection Improvement (Safety)",
  "Roadside / Median Improvement (Safety)",
  "Pedestrian Facilities",
  "Pavement Treatments (Safety)"
)

# Join category info onto segment_year_data
segment_year_typed <- segment_year_data %>%
  left_join(
    treated_typed_segments %>% 
      filter(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY %in% key_categories),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    work_category = case_when(
      !is.na(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY) ~ PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY,
      treated_safety == FALSE ~ "Control",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(work_category))

# Calculate control change separately first
control_change_val <- segment_year_typed %>%
  filter(work_category == "Control") %>%
  group_by(period) %>%
  summarise(avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = avg_crash_rate) %>%
  mutate(change = after - before) %>%
  pull(change)

cat("Control change:", control_change_val, "\n")

# Now run DiD for each category
did_by_category <- segment_year_typed %>%
  filter(work_category %in% key_categories) %>%
  group_by(work_category, period) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = c(avg_crash_rate, n_obs)) %>%
  mutate(
    change = avg_crash_rate_after - avg_crash_rate_before,
    did_effect = change - control_change_val
  )

as.data.frame(did_by_category)

ggplot(did_by_category, aes(x = reorder(work_category, did_effect), 
                            y = did_effect, 
                            fill = did_effect < 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(did_effect, 2)), 
            hjust = ifelse(did_by_category$did_effect < 0, 1.2, -0.2),
            size = 4) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "tomato"),
                    labels = c("FALSE" = "Crash increase", "TRUE" = "Crash reduction")) +
  labs(
    title = "Safety Project Impact on Crash Rates by Work Category",
    subtitle = "DiD effect vs control | Negative = crash reduction relative to control",
    x = NULL,
    y = "DiD Effect (crashes per MVMT vs control)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

### Very interesting stuff here, basically seems to be showing me that only intersection
### improvement projects are making things safer where as other safety projects make 
### little changes or even make things worse. 
```

```{r}
##################################
### What makes roads Safer? (RF)
##################################

# Build a modeling dataset at the segment level
# Join all the info we have onto segments that have crash rate changes

# First create a before/after crash rate per segment
segment_change <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(period %in% c("before", "after")) %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, period) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = avg_crash_rate) %>%
  mutate(
    crash_rate_change = after - before,
    improved = crash_rate_change < 0  # TRUE = got safer
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(before), !is.na(after))

library(randomForest)

# Build clean modeling dataset
model_df <- segment_change %>%
  left_join(
    analysis_df %>%
      st_drop_geometry() %>%
      select(
        ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID,
        ADT_TOTAL_NBR,
        SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR,
        FUNCTION_CLASS_CD,
        SPEED_LIMIT_NBR,
        MEDIAN_TYPE_CD,
        LANES_NBR,
        LANE_WIDTH_NBR,
        SHOULDER_TYPE_CD,
        SHOULDER_TOTAL_WIDTH_LT,
        SHOULDER_TOTAL_WIDTH_RT,
        SURFACE_TYPE_LEFT_CD,
        AREA_TYPE,
        DIVIDED_HWY_IND.x,
        PCR_NBR,
        AVG_IRI,
        TOT_PCR_DEDUCT_NBR,
        TOT_CRACK_DEDUCT_NBR,
        TOT_RUT_DEDUCT_NBR,
        RUTTING_VALUE,
        POTHOLE_VALUE,
        PATCHING_VALUE,
        crashes_per_MVMT
      ),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    treated_typed_segments %>%
      select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY = replace_na(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, "No Project"),
    PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY = as.factor(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY),
    FUNCTION_CLASS_CD = as.factor(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD),
    MEDIAN_TYPE_CD = as.factor(MEDIAN_TYPE_CD),
    SHOULDER_TYPE_CD = as.factor(SHOULDER_TYPE_CD),
    SURFACE_TYPE_LEFT_CD = as.factor(SURFACE_TYPE_LEFT_CD),
    AREA_TYPE = as.factor(AREA_TYPE),
    DIVIDED_HWY_IND.x = as.factor(DIVIDED_HWY_IND.x),
    improved = as.factor(ifelse(crash_rate_change < 0, "Improved", "Worsened"))
  )

# Check outcome balance
table(model_df$improved)

# Check missingness
colSums(is.na(model_df %>% select(-ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, -before, -after, 
                                  -crash_rate_change)))


# Drop high-NA columns, keep clean ones
rf_df <- model_df %>%
  select(
    improved,
    ADT_TOTAL_NBR,
    SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR,
    FUNCTION_CLASS_CD,
    SPEED_LIMIT_NBR,
    LANES_NBR,
    SURFACE_TYPE_LEFT_CD,
    DIVIDED_HWY_IND.x,
    PCR_NBR,
    TOT_PCR_DEDUCT_NBR,
    TOT_CRACK_DEDUCT_NBR,
    TOT_RUT_DEDUCT_NBR,
    crashes_per_MVMT,
    PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY
  ) %>%
  # Drop rows with any remaining NAs
  drop_na()

# Check what's left
nrow(rf_df)
table(rf_df$improved)

# Train random forest
set.seed(42)
rf_model <- randomForest(
  improved ~ .,
  data = rf_df,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  sampsize = c(Improved = 3000, Worsened = 3000)  # balance the classes
)

print(rf_model)

# Extract feature importance
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Variable") %>%
  arrange(desc(MeanDecreaseGini))

# Plot
ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(Variable, MeanDecreaseGini), 
                          y = MeanDecreaseGini)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "What Predicts Whether a Road Segment Improves or Worsens?",
    subtitle = "Random Forest Feature Importance (Mean Decrease Gini)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Importance Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Compare characteristics between improved and worsened segments directly
rf_df %>%
  group_by(improved) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_speed_limit = mean(SPEED_LIMIT_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_PCR = mean(PCR_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_crack_deduct = mean(TOT_CRACK_DEDUCT_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_rut_deduct = mean(TOT_RUT_DEDUCT_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_lanes = mean(LANES_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_baseline_crash_rate = mean(crashes_per_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Bin segments by their baseline crash rate and see improvement rates
rf_df %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_bin = cut(crashes_per_MVMT,
                       breaks = c(0, 10, 25, 50, 100, Inf),
                       labels = c("Very Low (0-10)", "Low (10-25)", 
                                  "Medium (25-50)", "High (50-100)", 
                                  "Very High (100+)"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(baseline_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n(),
    pct_improved = mean(improved == "Improved") * 100,
    avg_change = mean(as.numeric(as.character(
      factor(improved, levels = c("Improved","Worsened"), labels = c(-1,1))
    )), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Summary plot of all DiD effects including the "all projects" result
all_effects <- did_by_category %>%
  select(work_category, did_effect) %>%
  bind_rows(
    data.frame(
      work_category = "All Safety Projects (Overall)",
      did_effect = did_safety_effect_value
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    significant = work_category == "Intersection Improvement (Safety)",
    work_category = str_wrap(work_category, width = 30)
  )

ggplot(all_effects, aes(x = reorder(work_category, did_effect),
                        y = did_effect,
                        fill = did_effect < 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(did_effect, 2)),
            hjust = ifelse(all_effects$did_effect < 0, 1.2, -0.2),
            size = 5.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "tomato"),
    labels = c("TRUE" = "Crash reduction", "FALSE" = "Crash increase")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Which Safety Projects Actually Reduce Crashes?",
    subtitle = "Difference-in-Differences effect vs matched control segments",
    x = NULL,
    y = "DiD Effect (crashes per MVMT)",
    fill = NULL,
    caption = "Negative values indicate crash reduction relative to control roads"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")





### High Risk Model


# Build a high risk indicator and profile what those segments look like
high_risk_profile <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    high_risk = crashes_per_MVMT >= quantile(crashes_per_MVMT, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    received_safety_investment = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% 
      treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  ) %>%
  group_by(high_risk) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n(),
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_speed_limit = mean(SPEED_LIMIT_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_PCR = mean(PCR_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_length = mean(SEGMENT_LENGTH_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_lanes = mean(LANES_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_IRI = mean(AVG_IRI, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_crack_deduct = mean(TOT_CRACK_DEDUCT_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_rut_deduct = mean(TOT_RUT_DEDUCT_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_received_investment = mean(received_safety_investment) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

as.data.frame(high_risk_profile)

# Also look at functional class breakdown of high risk segments
analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(high_risk = crashes_per_MVMT >= quantile(crashes_per_MVMT, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, high_risk) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = high_risk, values_from = n, names_prefix = "high_risk_") %>%
  mutate(pct_high_risk = high_risk_TRUE / (high_risk_TRUE + high_risk_FALSE) * 100) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_high_risk)) %>%
  as.data.frame()






# Build severity profile by functional class and risk level
severity_profile <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    high_risk = crashes_per_MVMT >= quantile(crashes_per_MVMT, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, high_risk) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n(),
    total_crashes = sum(total_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_fatal = sum(fatal_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_injury = sum(injury_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    fatal_rate = sum(fatal_crashes) / sum(total_crashes) * 100,
    injury_rate = sum(injury_crashes) / sum(total_crashes) * 100,
    crashes_per_segment = mean(total_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, high_risk) %>%
  as.data.frame()

print(severity_profile)

# Also get a clean high risk vs normal summary by severity
analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    high_risk = crashes_per_MVMT >= quantile(crashes_per_MVMT, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(high_risk) %>%
  summarise(
    total_crashes = sum(total_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_fatal = sum(fatal_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_injury = sum(injury_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_of_all_fatals = NA,  # will compute below
    fatal_rate = sum(fatal_crashes) / sum(total_crashes) * 100,
    injury_rate = sum(injury_crashes) / sum(total_crashes) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_of_all_fatals = total_fatal / sum(total_fatal) * 100,
    pct_of_all_injuries = total_injury / sum(total_injury) * 100,
    pct_of_all_crashes = total_crashes / sum(total_crashes) * 100
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()
```

```{r}
#############################
### What is more dangerous? 
#############################

fc_summary <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n(),
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_crashes = sum(total_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_fatal = sum(fatal_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_injury = sum(injury_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_crashes_per_MVMT = round(mean(crashes_per_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    fatal_rate_pct = round(sum(fatal_crashes) / sum(total_crashes) * 100, 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_of_all_crashes = round(total_crashes / sum(total_crashes) * 100, 1),
    pct_of_all_fatals = round(total_fatal / sum(total_fatal) * 100, 1),
    avg_crashes_per_segment = round(total_crashes / n_segments, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD)

as.data.frame(fc_summary)

analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    received_safety = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID
  ) %>%
  filter(received_safety == TRUE) %>%
  group_by(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD) %>%
  summarise(
    n_treated_segments = n(),
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_crashes_per_MVMT = round(mean(crashes_per_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    total_fatal = sum(fatal_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    fatal_rate_pct = round(sum(fatal_crashes) / sum(total_crashes) * 100, 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_of_safety_work = round(n_treated_segments / sum(n_treated_segments) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_treated_segments)) %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Build the investment vs need comparison directly
investment_vs_need <- fc_summary %>%
  left_join(
    analysis_df %>%
      st_drop_geometry() %>%
      mutate(received_safety = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% 
               treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
      group_by(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD) %>%
      summarise(
        n_treated = sum(received_safety),
        pct_treated = round(mean(received_safety) * 100, 1),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = "FUNCTION_CLASS_CD"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    fc_label = case_when(
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 1 ~ "1 - Interstate",
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 2 ~ "2 - Freeway",
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 3 ~ "3 - Major Arterial",
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 4 ~ "4 - Minor Arterial",
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 5 ~ "5 - Collector",
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 6 ~ "6 - Minor Collector",
      FUNCTION_CLASS_CD == 7 ~ "7 - Local"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(fc_label, n_segments, pct_treated, avg_crashes_per_MVMT, 
         pct_of_all_fatals, fatal_rate_pct, avg_ADT)

as.data.frame(investment_vs_need)
```

```{r}
############################################
### Cincinnati / Hamilton County Analysis
############################################

# Check what county identifier looks like in your data
unique(analysis_df$COUNTY_CD.x) %>% head(20)

# Hamilton County code in Ohio is 31
# Correct filter using abbreviation
cincinnati_df <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(COUNTY_CD.x == "HAM")

nrow(cincinnati_df)

# Verify it worked
head(cincinnati_df$COUNTY_CD.x)

# Also filter segment_year_data to Cincinnati
cincinnati_year <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% cincinnati_df$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID)

# Quick profile of Cincinnati roads
cincinnati_df %>%
  group_by(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n(),
    avg_ADT = mean(ADT_TOTAL_NBR, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crashes_per_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_fatal = sum(fatal_crashes, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_treated = mean(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% 
                         treated_safety_ids$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD) %>%
  as.data.frame()



COL_ORANGE  <- "#f0883e"
COL_BLUE    <- "#58a6ff"
COL_GREEN   <- "#3fb950"
COL_RED     <- "#f85149"
COL_YELLOW  <- "#d29922"
COL_MUTED   <- "#8b949e"
COL_SURFACE <- "#161b22"

theme_odot <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "#0d1117", color = NA),
      panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "#161b22", color = NA),
      panel.grid.major   = element_line(color = "#30363d", linewidth = 0.4),
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.border       = element_rect(color = "#30363d", fill = NA),
      text               = element_text(color = "#e6edf3"),
      axis.text          = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 10),
      axis.title         = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 11),
      plot.title         = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 14,
                                        face = "bold",
                                        margin = ggplot2::margin(b = 4)),
      plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 10,
                                        margin = ggplot2::margin(b = 16)),
      plot.caption       = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 9,
                                        margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 12)),
      legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "#161b22", color = NA),
      legend.text        = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 10),
      legend.title       = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 10),
      strip.text         = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 10),
      plot.margin        = ggplot2::margin(16, 16, 16, 16)
    )
}

cincinnati_fc_plot <- data.frame(
  fc_label      = factor(c("1-Interstate", "2-Freeway", "3-Major Art.",
                           "4-Minor Art.", "5-Collector", "7-Local"),
                         levels = c("1-Interstate", "2-Freeway", "3-Major Art.",
                                    "4-Minor Art.", "5-Collector", "7-Local")),
  avg_crash_rate = c(18.66, 11.88, 33.84, 37.29, 40.23, 41.23),
  pct_treated    = c(4.78, 6.35, 14.16, 6.52, 2.18, 0.42),
  total_fatal    = c(39, 4, 75, 69, 47, 15),
  n_segments     = c(691, 189, 883, 966, 1146, 710)
) %>%
  mutate(
    bar_color = case_when(
      fc_label %in% c("1-Interstate", "2-Freeway") ~ COL_BLUE,
      fc_label == "3-Major Art."                   ~ COL_YELLOW,
      fc_label %in% c("4-Minor Art.", "5-Collector") ~ COL_RED,
      fc_label == "7-Local"                         ~ COL_ORANGE
    ),
    pct_of_fatals = round(total_fatal / sum(total_fatal) * 100, 1)
  )

# ── Plot A: Investment Rate vs Crash Rate (bubble) ───────────────────────────
p_bubble <- ggplot(cincinnati_fc_plot,
                   aes(x = pct_treated, y = avg_crash_rate,
                       size = pct_of_fatals, fill = bar_color)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, alpha = 0.85,
             color = "#0d1117", stroke = 1.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = fc_label),
            vjust = -1.5, size = 5.5, color = "#e6edf3") +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -0.3, xmax = 4,
           ymin = 35, ymax = 44,
           fill = COL_RED, alpha = 0.06,
           color = COL_RED, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.8, y = 43.5,
           label = "High risk, low investment",
           color = COL_RED, size = 5.0, fontface = "italic") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(5, 20),
                        name = "% of Local Fatalities") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.5, 17),
                     labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Cincinnati: Investment Gap by Road Class",
    subtitle = "Bubble size = share of Hamilton County fatalities",
    x        = "% of Segments Receiving Safety Investment",
    y        = "Avg Crash Rate (per MVMT)"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_bubble

# ── Plot B: Side by side investment vs crash rate bars ───────────────────────
p_bars <- cincinnati_fc_plot %>%
  select(fc_label, avg_crash_rate, pct_treated) %>%
  pivot_longer(-fc_label,
               names_to  = "metric",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = case_when(
    metric == "avg_crash_rate" ~ "Avg Crash Rate per MVMT",
    metric == "pct_treated"    ~ "% Segments Receiving Safety Work"
  )) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fc_label, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Avg Crash Rate per MVMT"          = COL_ORANGE,
    "% Segments Receiving Safety Work" = COL_BLUE
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Risk vs Investment — Hamilton County",
    subtitle = "Collectors and local roads are the most underserved",
    x = NULL, y = "Value", fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

p_bars

# ── Plot C: Fatal share bar ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_fatal <- ggplot(cincinnati_fc_plot,
                  aes(x = reorder(fc_label, -pct_of_fatals),
                      y = pct_of_fatals,
                      fill = bar_color)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct_of_fatals, "%")),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5.5, color = "#e6edf3") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 40),
                     labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Hamilton County Fatalities by Road Class",
    subtitle = "% of all Hamilton County traffic fatalities 2020–2024",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "% of Local Fatalities"
  ) +
  theme_odot()

p_fatal

# ── Combined panel ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(patchwork)

cincinnati_panel <- (p_bubble | p_fatal) / p_bars +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Cincinnati (Hamilton County) — Safety Investment Analysis",
    subtitle = "4,585 road segments | 249 total fatalities 2020–2024",
    caption  = "Source: ODOT Crash Data 2020–2024 · Road Inventory",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#0d1117", color = NA),
      plot.title      = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 11),
      plot.caption    = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 9)
    )
  )

cincinnati_panel
```

```{r}
#####################
### Testing DID
#####################

# Test parallel trends using pre-period years only
# We need yearly crash rates for 2020 and 2021 (both "before" years)

parallel_trends_test <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(CRASH_YR %in% c(2020, 2021)) %>%
  group_by(treated_safety, CRASH_YR) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_crash_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(parallel_trends_test)

# Visualize
ggplot(parallel_trends_test,
       aes(x = CRASH_YR, y = avg_crash_rate,
           color = treated_safety, group = treated_safety)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = COL_MUTED, "TRUE" = COL_BLUE),
                     labels = c("FALSE" = "Control", "TRUE" = "Treated")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2020, 2021)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Parallel Trends Test: Pre-Treatment Period",
    subtitle = "Lines should be parallel for DiD assumption to hold",
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Avg Crash Rate per MVMT",
    color    = NULL
  ) +
  theme_odot()




# Check how many segments appear in both before and after
period_counts <- segment_year_data %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    has_before = any(period == "before"),
    has_after  = any(period == "after"),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

table(period_counts$has_before, period_counts$has_after,
      dnn = c("Has Before", "Has After"))


# Compare raw vs log-transformed crash rates
segment_year_data %>%
  filter(crash_rate_MVMT > 0, crash_rate_MVMT < 500) %>%
  mutate(log_rate = log(crash_rate_MVMT)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(crash_rate_MVMT, log_rate),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 80, fill = COL_BLUE, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title    = "Crash Rate Distribution: Raw vs Log Transformed",
    subtitle = "Log transformation may better satisfy model assumptions",
    x = NULL, y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_odot()

# If log looks more normal, rerun DiD with log outcome
balanced_segments <- balanced_segments %>%
  mutate(log_crash_rate = log(crash_rate_MVMT + 0.01))

did_model_log <- felm(
  log_crash_rate ~ treated_safety * I(period == "after") |
    ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID + CRASH_YR |
    0 |
    ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID,
  data = balanced_segments %>% filter(is.finite(log_crash_rate))
)

summary(did_model_log)




### RESULTS

## Parralell Line test shows no extreme converging or diverging trend so that fine

## 
```

```{r}
############################################
### ODOT Safety Investment Analysis
### Progress Report Visualizations
### Xavier University Senior Capstone 2026
############################################

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)  # install.packages("patchwork") if needed

# ── Shared theme ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


theme_odot <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "#0d1117", color = NA),
      panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "#161b22", color = NA),
      panel.grid.major   = element_line(color = "#30363d", linewidth = 0.4),
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.border       = element_rect(color = "#30363d", fill = NA),
      text               = element_text(color = "#e6edf3"),
      axis.text          = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 10),
      axis.title         = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 11),
      plot.title         = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 14,
                                        face = "bold",
                                        margin = ggplot2::margin(b = 4)),
      plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 10,
                                        margin = ggplot2::margin(b = 16)),
      plot.caption       = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 9,
                                        margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 12)),
      legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "#161b22", color = NA),
      legend.text        = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 10),
      legend.title       = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 10),
      strip.text         = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 10),
      plot.margin        = ggplot2::margin(16, 16, 16, 16)
    )
}

# Color palette
COL_ORANGE  <- "#f0883e"
COL_BLUE    <- "#58a6ff"
COL_GREEN   <- "#3fb950"
COL_RED     <- "#f85149"
COL_YELLOW  <- "#d29922"
COL_MUTED   <- "#8b949e"
COL_SURFACE <- "#161b22"


############################################
### FIGURE 1: Difference-in-Differences
### Before vs After — Matched Control
############################################

did_plot_data <- data.frame(
  period  = factor(c("Before", "After", "Before", "After"),
                   levels = c("Before", "After")),
  group   = c("Control (Matched ADT)",  "Control (Matched ADT)",
              "Treated (Safety-Funded)", "Treated (Safety-Funded)"),
  rate    = c(10.6, 10.3, 7.65, 7.42)
)

fig1 <- ggplot(did_plot_data,
               aes(x = period, y = rate, color = group, group = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_label(aes(label = round(rate, 2)),
             nudge_x = 0.07, show.legend = FALSE,
             fill = "#161b22", size = 5.5,
             linewidth = 0)  + # <-- replaces label.size = 0
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control (Matched ADT)"   = COL_MUTED,
                                "Treated (Safety-Funded)" = COL_BLUE)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Difference-in-Differences: Safety Project Impact",
    subtitle = "Segments matched on ADT volume | DiD effect = +0.07 (p = 0.908, not significant)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    color    = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: ODOT Crash Data 2020–2024"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

fig1


############################################
### FIGURE 2: DiD Effect by Project Type
############################################

category_data <- data.frame(
  category   = c("Intersection Imp. (Safety)",
                 "Pedestrian Facilities",
                 "Roadway Minor Rehab",
                 "Roadside/Median Imp.",
                 "Pavement Treatments (Safety)",
                 "Traffic Control (Safety)"),
  did_effect = c(-6.54, 0.14, 0.34, 1.15, 1.65, 2.49),
  n_segments = c(697, 277, 1266, 664, 263, 2739)
)

fig2 <- ggplot(category_data,
               aes(x = reorder(category, -did_effect),
                   y = did_effect,
                   fill = did_effect < 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = COL_MUTED, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(did_effect, 2),
                hjust = ifelse(did_effect < 0, 1.3, -0.3)),
            color = "#e6edf3", size = 5.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = COL_GREEN, "FALSE" = COL_RED),
                    labels = c("TRUE" = "Crash reduction",
                               "FALSE" = "Crash increase")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Which Safety Projects Actually Reduce Crashes?",
    subtitle = "DiD effect vs matched control segments | Negative = crash reduction",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "DiD Effect (crashes per MVMT vs control)",
    fill     = NULL,
    caption  = "Only categories with 200+ treated segments shown"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

fig2


############################################
### FIGURE 3: Event Study — All Projects
############################################

event_data <- data.frame(
  event_time     = -2:2,
  avg_crash_rate = c(8.10, 7.64, 6.74, 6.80, 7.42),
  se             = c(0.755, 0.689, 0.428, 0.409, 0.532)
)

fig3 <- ggplot(event_data, aes(x = event_time, y = avg_crash_rate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = avg_crash_rate - 1.96 * se,
                  ymax = avg_crash_rate + 1.96 * se),
              fill = COL_BLUE, alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3, color = COL_BLUE) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = COL_BLUE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = COL_RED, linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.15, y = 8.3,
           label = "Construction\nComplete",
           color = COL_RED, hjust = 0, size = 5.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = -2:2) +
  labs(
    title    = "Event Study: Crash Rates Around Safety Project Completion",
    subtitle = "Treated segments only | t=0 is completion year | 95% CI shown",
    x        = "Years Relative to Project Completion",
    y        = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    caption  = "n = 2,046–2,662 segments per time point"
  ) +
  theme_odot()

fig3


############################################
### FIGURE 4: 2021 Cohort — Lag Test
############################################

cohort_data <- data.frame(
  event_time     = -1:3,
  avg_crash_rate = c(6.5, 6.9, 7.2, 8.3, 9.0)
)

fig4 <- ggplot(cohort_data, aes(x = event_time, y = avg_crash_rate)) +
  geom_area(fill = COL_RED, alpha = 0.1) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3, color = COL_RED) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = COL_RED) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = COL_MUTED, linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.15, y = 9.2,
           label = "Construction\nComplete",
           color = COL_MUTED, hjust = 0, size = 5.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = -1:3) +
  labs(
    title    = "2021 Cohort: No Lag Effect Found",
    subtitle = "Crash rates increase steadily post-completion — no delayed safety benefit",
    x        = "Years Relative to 2021 Project Completion",
    y        = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    caption  = "Only segments completing construction in 2021 | Extended to t=+3"
  ) +
  theme_odot()

fig4


############################################
### FIGURE 5: Fatalities by Functional Class
############################################

fc_data <- data.frame(
  fc_label      = factor(c("1-Interstate","2-Freeway","3-Major Art.",
                           "4-Minor Art.","5-Collector","6-Minor Coll.","7-Local"),
                         levels = c("1-Interstate","2-Freeway","3-Major Art.",
                                    "4-Minor Art.","5-Collector",
                                    "6-Minor Coll.","7-Local")),
  pct_fatals    = c(8.1, 2.3, 19.3, 24.0, 28.5, 4.9, 12.8),
  crash_rate    = c(11.3, 8.0, 23.7, 25.6, 25.0, 22.6, 36.5),
  fatal_rate    = c(0.47, 0.48, 0.40, 0.43, 0.58, 0.82, 0.58),
  pct_treated   = c(9.8, 12.0, 14.6, 6.1, 3.6, 4.0, 1.2),
  avg_adt       = c(29301, 14841, 13202, 8359, 3722, 1111, 1034)
)

# Highlight FC 4+5 as the key finding
fc_data <- fc_data %>%
  mutate(
    highlight = fc_label %in% c("4-Minor Art.", "5-Collector"),
    bar_color = case_when(
      fc_label %in% c("1-Interstate", "2-Freeway") ~ COL_BLUE,
      fc_label %in% c("4-Minor Art.", "5-Collector") ~ COL_RED,
      fc_label == "7-Local" ~ COL_ORANGE,
      TRUE ~ COL_YELLOW
    )
  )

fig5 <- ggplot(fc_data, aes(x = fc_label)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = pct_fatals, fill = bar_color),
           width = 0.6, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_line(aes(y = crash_rate, group = 1),
            color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(aes(y = crash_rate),
             color = "white", size = 5.5) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name     = "% of All Fatalities (bars)",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~., name = "Avg Crash Rate per MVMT (line)")
  ) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 3.55, xmax = 5.45,
           ymin = -1, ymax = 31,
           fill = COL_RED, alpha = 0.06) +
  annotate("text", x = 4.5, y = 33,
           label = "52.5% of all\nfatalities",
           color = COL_RED, size = 5.5, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title    = "Where Is the Harm? Fatalities & Crash Rates by Road Class",
    subtitle = "FC 4 + FC 5 roads account for more than half of all Ohio traffic fatalities",
    x        = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: ODOT Crash Data 2020–2024 · 4,466 total fatalities"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "white"))

fig5


#Minor Arterial roads are a bridge between high-speed highways and local 
#neighborhood streets. They are designed to carry moderate-length trips through 
#a region at slower speeds

#Collector roads are low-to-moderate capacity roads that "collect" traffic from 
#local residential streets and funnel it onto major arterials or highways. 

############################################
### FIGURE 6: The Investment Gap
### % Treated vs Crash Rate (bubble = fatality share)
############################################

fig6 <- ggplot(fc_data,
               aes(x = pct_treated, y = crash_rate,
                   size = pct_fatals, fill = bar_color)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, alpha = 0.8, color = "#0d1117", stroke = 1.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = fc_label),
            vjust = -1.4, size = 5.5, color = "#e6edf3") +
  # Danger zone annotation
  annotate("rect", xmin = -0.5, xmax = 5,
           ymin = 28, ymax = 40,
           fill = COL_RED, alpha = 0.05,
           linetype = "dashed", color = COL_RED, linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 39,
           label = "High risk, low investment",
           color = COL_RED, size = 3, fontface = "italic") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 18), name = "% of All Fatalities") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.5, 17),
                     labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "The Investment Gap: Where Safety Funding Goes vs Where It's Needed",
    subtitle = "Bubble size = share of all fatalities | Local roads sit in the danger zone",
    x        = "% of Segments Receiving Safety Investment",
    y        = "Average Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    caption  = "Ideal: high investment AND low crash rate (upper left = over-invested, lower right = under-invested)"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

fig6


############################################
### FIGURE 7: Investment Gap Bar Chart
### Side-by-side: % treated vs crash rate
############################################

gap_long <- fc_data %>%
  select(fc_label, pct_treated, crash_rate) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pct_treated, crash_rate),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    metric = case_when(
      metric == "pct_treated" ~ "% Segments Receiving Safety Work",
      metric == "crash_rate"  ~ "Avg Crash Rate per MVMT"
    )
  )

fig7 <- ggplot(gap_long,
               aes(x = fc_label, y = value,
                   fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "% Segments Receiving Safety Work" = COL_BLUE,
    "Avg Crash Rate per MVMT"          = COL_ORANGE
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Safety Investment Rate vs Road Risk by Functional Class",
    subtitle = "Investment (blue) vs crash rate (orange) — gap is largest for local roads",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Value",
    fill     = NULL,
    caption  = "Local roads (FC 7): 1.2% investment rate despite highest crash rate (36.5/MVMT)"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

fig7


############################################
### FIGURE 8: High Risk Road Profile
### Improved vs Worsened segments
############################################

profile_data <- data.frame(
  group    = c("Improved", "Worsened"),
  avg_adt  = c(11666, 11086),
  avg_pcr  = c(81.6, 82.1),
  avg_spd  = c(40.6, 41.6),
  baseline = c(39.1, 31.4),
  n        = c(15667, 23473)
)

profile_long <- profile_data %>%
  select(group, avg_adt, avg_pcr, avg_spd, baseline) %>%
  pivot_longer(-group, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = case_when(
    metric == "avg_adt"  ~ "Avg ADT (Traffic Volume)",
    metric == "avg_pcr"  ~ "Avg PCR (Pavement Score)",
    metric == "avg_spd"  ~ "Avg Speed Limit (mph)",
    metric == "baseline" ~ "Baseline Crash Rate (per MVMT)"
  ))

fig8 <- ggplot(profile_long,
               aes(x = group, y = value, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Improved" = COL_BLUE,
                               "Worsened" = COL_RED)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Road Characteristics: Improved vs Worsened Segments",
    subtitle = "Baseline crash rate is the key differentiator — all other characteristics nearly identical",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL,
    fill     = NULL,
    caption  = "Improved n=15,667 | Worsened n=23,473 | Improvement defined as crash rate decline before→after"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text      = element_text(size = 9, color = "#8b949e")
  )

fig8
```

```{r}
############################################
### Case Study: Find Candidate Projects
############################################

case_study_candidates <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  inner_join(
    projects_safety %>%
      select(NLF_ID, CTL_BEGIN, CTL_END, completion_year,
             PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST,
             COUNTY_NME, DISTRICT_NBR, PROJECT_NME,
             END_CONSTR_MILESTONE_DT),
    by = "NLF_ID",
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) %>%
  filter(
    CTL_BEGIN_NBR <= CTL_END.y,  # project end >= segment start
    CTL_END_NBR >= CTL_BEGIN.y   # project start <= segment end
  ) %>%
  filter(
    PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY %in% c(
      "Intersection Improvement (Safety)",
      "Traffic Control (Safety)",
      "Roadway Minor Rehab"
    )
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    segment_year_data %>%
      group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
      summarise(
        before_rate = mean(crash_rate_MVMT[period == "before"], na.rm = TRUE),
        after_rate  = mean(crash_rate_MVMT[period == "after"],  na.rm = TRUE),
        change      = after_rate - before_rate,
        .groups     = "drop"
      ),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(change)) %>%
  select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, NLF_ID, PROJECT_NME, COUNTY_NME,
         COUNTY_CD.x, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, completion_year,
         EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST, FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, ADT_TOTAL_NBR,
         SPEED_LIMIT_NBR, before_rate, after_rate, change)

# Check Hamilton County availability
case_study_candidates %>%
  filter(COUNTY_CD.x == "HAM") %>%
  count(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, sort = TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame()
```

```{r}
# Best intersection improvement — biggest crash reduction
best_intersection <- case_study_candidates %>%
  filter(COUNTY_CD.x == "HAM",
         PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY == "Intersection Improvement (Safety)") %>%
  slice_min(change, n = 3) %>%
  select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PROJECT_NME, COUNTY_NME,
         completion_year, before_rate, after_rate, change,
         EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST, FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, ADT_TOTAL_NBR)

# Best traffic control — biggest crash increase (most interesting negative case)
best_traffic <- case_study_candidates %>%
  filter(COUNTY_CD.x == "HAM",
         PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY == "Traffic Control (Safety)") %>%
  slice_max(change, n = 3) %>%
  select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PROJECT_NME, COUNTY_NME,
         completion_year, before_rate, after_rate, change,
         EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST, FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, ADT_TOTAL_NBR)

# Best rehab — closest to zero change (true null result)
best_rehab <- case_study_candidates %>%
  filter(COUNTY_CD.x == "HAM",
         PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY == "Roadway Minor Rehab") %>%
  mutate(abs_change = abs(change)) %>%
  slice_min(abs_change, n = 3) %>%
  select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PROJECT_NME, COUNTY_NME,
         completion_year, before_rate, after_rate, change,
         EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST, FUNCTION_CLASS_CD, ADT_TOTAL_NBR)

cat("=== INTERSECTION IMPROVEMENT CANDIDATES ===\n")
as.data.frame(best_intersection)

cat("\n=== TRAFFIC CONTROL CANDIDATES ===\n")
as.data.frame(best_traffic)

cat("\n=== ROADWAY REHAB CANDIDATES ===\n")
as.data.frame(best_rehab)
```

```{r}
############################################
### Case Study Profiles
############################################

# Define our three chosen segments
case_ids <- c(
  "2024_000000000018030",  # Intersection - Plainfield Roundabouts
  "2024_000000000271525",  # Traffic Control - Eastern Corridor
  "2024_000000000270710"   # Rehab - SR 32/SR 125
)

case_labels <- c(
  "2024_000000000018030" = "Plainfield Rd Roundabouts\n(Intersection Improvement)",
  "2024_000000000271525" = "Eastern Corridor VAR TSG\n(Traffic Control)",
  "2024_000000000270710" = "SR 32/SR 125\n(Roadway Minor Rehab)"
)

case_colors <- c(
  "2024_000000000018030" = COL_GREEN,
  "2024_000000000271525" = COL_RED,
  "2024_000000000270710" = COL_MUTED
)

# Pull yearly crash rates for each case study segment
case_yearly <- segment_year_data %>%
  filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% case_ids) %>%
  left_join(
    case_study_candidates %>%
      filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% case_ids) %>%
      distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, completion_year, PROJECT_NME,
               PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST,
               before_rate, after_rate, change),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label     = case_labels[ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID],
    seg_color = case_colors[ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID]
  )


case_yearly <- case_yearly %>%
  left_join(
    analysis_df %>%
      st_drop_geometry() %>%
      select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, FUNCTION_CLASS_CD) %>%
      rename(FC = FUNCTION_CLASS_CD),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(FUNCTION_CLASS_CD = FC) %>%
  select(-FC)

# Check we have data
case_yearly %>%
  group_by(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, label) %>%
  summarise(
    years     = paste(sort(unique(CRASH_YR)), collapse = ", "),
    min_rate  = round(min(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    max_rate  = round(max(crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()
```

```{r}
############################################
### Case Study Visualizations
############################################

# ── Individual yearly trend plots ─────────────────────────────────────────────

plot_case_study <- function(seg_id) {
  
  seg_data <- case_yearly %>%
    filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID == seg_id)
  
  comp_year  <- unique(seg_data$completion_year)
  proj_name  <- unique(seg_data$PROJECT_NME)
  work_cat   <- unique(seg_data$PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY)
  cost       <- scales::dollar(unique(seg_data$EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST))
  before     <- round(unique(seg_data$before_rate), 1)
  after      <- round(unique(seg_data$after_rate), 1)
  chg        <- round(unique(seg_data$change), 1)
  seg_color  <- unique(seg_data$seg_color)
  
  chg_label  <- ifelse(chg < 0,
                       paste0("▼ ", abs(chg), " per MVMT"),
                       paste0("▲ ", abs(chg), " per MVMT"))
  chg_color  <- ifelse(chg < 0, COL_GREEN, COL_RED)
  
  ggplot(seg_data, aes(x = CRASH_YR, y = crash_rate_MVMT)) +
    geom_area(fill = seg_color, alpha = 0.1) +
    geom_line(color = seg_color, linewidth = 1.3) +
    geom_point(color = seg_color, size = 4) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = comp_year, linetype = "dashed",
               color = COL_ORANGE, linewidth = 0.8) +
    annotate("text", x = comp_year + 0.08,
             y = max(seg_data$crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
             label = paste0("Completed\n", comp_year),
             color = COL_ORANGE, hjust = 0, size = 5.5) +
    annotate("label",
             x = mean(range(seg_data$CRASH_YR)),
             y = max(seg_data$crash_rate_MVMT, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.5,
             label = paste0("Before: ", before,
                            "\nAfter:  ", after,
                            "\nChange: ", chg_label),
             fill = "#1c2128", color = chg_color,
             size = 5.5, label.size = 0.5,
             label.padding = unit(0.4, "lines")) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(seg_data$CRASH_YR)) +
    labs(
      title    = proj_name,
      subtitle = paste0(work_cat, " | Cost: ", cost),
      x        = "Year",
      y        = "Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
      caption  = paste0("Segment ID: ", seg_id,
                        " | FC: ", unique(seg_data$FUNCTION_CLASS_CD),
                        " | ADT: ", 
                        scales::comma(unique(seg_data$ADT_TOTAL_NBR)))
    ) +
    theme_odot()
}

# Generate all three plots
p_intersection <- plot_case_study("2024_000000000018030")
p_traffic      <- plot_case_study("2024_000000000271525")
p_rehab        <- plot_case_study("2024_000000000270710")

p_intersection
p_traffic
p_rehab

# ── Summary comparison panel ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

summary_data <- case_study_candidates %>%
  filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% case_ids) %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    label     = case_labels[ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID],
    seg_color = case_colors[ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID],
    outcome   = case_when(
      change < 0 ~ "Improved",
      change > 0 ~ "Worsened",
      TRUE       ~ "No Change"
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(before_rate, after_rate),
               names_to = "period", values_to = "rate") %>%
  mutate(period = factor(ifelse(period == "before_rate", 
                                "Before", "After"),
                         levels = c("Before", "After")))

p_summary <- ggplot(summary_data,
                    aes(x = period, y = rate,
                        color = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID,
                        group = ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_label(aes(label = round(rate, 1)),
             nudge_x = 0.08, show.legend = FALSE,
             fill = "#161b22", size = 5.5, linewidth = 0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = case_colors,
                     labels = c(
                       "2024_000000000018030" = "Plainfield Roundabouts",
                       "2024_000000000271525" = "Eastern Corridor Traffic Control",
                       "2024_000000000270710" = "SR 32/SR 125 Rehab"
                     )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Case Study Comparison: Three Hamilton County Projects",
    subtitle = "Before vs after crash rates | Each line is one project",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Avg Crash Rate (per MVMT)",
    color    = NULL,
    caption  = "Hamilton County, Ohio | Safety-funded projects completing 2021–2023"
  ) +
  theme_odot() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_summary

# ── Combined panel ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
case_study_panel <- (p_intersection | p_traffic | p_rehab) / p_summary +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Hamilton County Case Studies: What Do Safety Projects Actually Do?",
    subtitle = "Three projects — three different outcomes",
    caption  = "Source: ODOT Construction Projects & Crash Data 2020–2024",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#0d1117", color = NA),
      plot.title      = element_text(color = "#e6edf3", size = 16,
                                     face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 11),
      plot.caption    = element_text(color = "#8b949e", size = 9)
    )
  )

case_study_panel

#ggsave("hamilton_case_studies.png", case_study_panel,
 #      width = 16, height = 10, dpi = 150, bg = "#0d1117")


# Build from scratch using ALL project types
hamilton_all_types <- analysis_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(COUNTY_CD.x == "HAM") %>%
  inner_join(
    projects_safety %>%
      select(NLF_ID, CTL_BEGIN, CTL_END, completion_year,
             PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST),
    by = "NLF_ID",
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) %>%
  filter(
    CTL_BEGIN_NBR <= CTL_END.y,
    CTL_END_NBR   >= CTL_BEGIN.y
  ) %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY, 
           completion_year, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Check all categories present
cat("Categories found:", n_distinct(hamilton_all_types$PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY), "\n")

# Join yearly data and summarise
segment_year_data %>%
  filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% 
           hamilton_all_types$ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID) %>%
  left_join(
    hamilton_all_types %>%
      select(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY),
    by = "ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID"
  ) %>%
  group_by(PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY) %>%
  summarise(
    n_segments = n_distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID),
    avg_before = round(mean(crash_rate_MVMT[period == "before"], 
                            na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    avg_after  = round(mean(crash_rate_MVMT[period == "after"],  
                            na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    change    = round(avg_after - avg_before, 3),
    direction = case_when(
      change < -0.5 ~ "Improved",
      change >  0.5 ~ "Worsened",
      TRUE          ~ "No Change"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(change) %>%
  as.data.frame()
```

```{r}
# Cost per unit of crash reduction
case_study_candidates %>%
  filter(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% case_ids) %>%
  distinct(ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    # Cost per crash rate point reduced
    cost_per_unit_reduction = EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST / abs(change),
    # Annualized cost effectiveness
    crashes_reduced_per_million = (abs(change) / 
                                  EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST) * 1e6
  ) %>%
  select(PROJECT_NME, PRIMARY_WORK_CATEGORY,
         EST_TOTAL_CONSTR_COST, change,
         cost_per_unit_reduction,
         crashes_reduced_per_million) %>%
  as.data.frame()
```

```{r}
# Add cost effectiveness to the summary comparison plot
cost_data <- data.frame(
  project = c("Plainfield\nRoundabouts",
              "Eastern Corridor\nTraffic Control",
              "SR 32/SR 125\nRehab"),
  cost_millions     = c(8.863, 0.337, 1.616),
  crashes_per_million = c(5.10, -44.34, 0.00),
  color = c(COL_GREEN, COL_RED, COL_MUTED)
)

p_cost <- ggplot(cost_data,
                 aes(x = reorder(project, crashes_per_million),
                     y = crashes_per_million,
                     fill = color)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = COL_MUTED, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(crashes_per_million,
                               "\nper $1M")),
            vjust = ifelse(cost_data$crashes_per_million >= 0,
                          -0.3, 1.3),
            color = "#e6edf3", size = 5.5) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = "Cost Effectiveness: Crashes Reduced per $1M Spent",
    subtitle = "Hamilton County case studies | Negative = crashes increased",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Crash Rate Units Reduced per $1M",
    caption  = "Positive = safety improvement per dollar | Negative = safety worsened per dollar"
  ) +
  theme_odot()

p_cost
```

```{r}
# Find more candidates excluding all projects we've already used
used_ids <- c(
  "2024_000000000018030",  # Plainfield Roundabouts
  "2024_000000000271525",  # Eastern Corridor VAR TSG
  "2024_000000000270710",  # SR 32/SR 125
  "2024_000000000270927",  # Cincinnati PSIP Pedestrian
  "2024_000000000119861",  # GR Type A Roadside
  "2024_000000000120118"   # Cincinnati Friction
)

new_candidates_3 <- hamilton_all_types %>%
  filter(!ROADWAY_INVENTORY_ID %in% used_ids) %>%
  left_join(
    projects_safety %>%
      select(NLF_ID, PROJECT_NME) %>%
      distinct(),
```
